This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: **Task 1**: Add comprehensive logging to the `tenex-edge` daemon. When running `tenex-edge daemon`, produce colorized output on stdout AND write the same to daemon.log simultaneously. Log all key daemon operations: routing to sessions, starting new agents (with reasons), ordinal creation (with reasons), subscriptions, relay events. Use 5 parallel Sonnet agents to decide what to log in each subsystem. **Task 2** (follow-up): User showed a screenshot of the output and said "this is very light on colors and on usefulness." Problems: (a) raw `[â†’relay]`/`[daemon]` lines from code still using `eprintln!` were cluttering output, (b) only the level badge was colored â€” message text and field values were plain white, (c) status heartbeats (kind:30315) were at INFO level causing noise every 30s. 2. Key Technical Concepts: - Rust `tracing` + `tracing-subscriber` crates for structured logging - `owo-colors` crate (already a dep) for ANSI terminal colors - Custom `FormatEvent` and field `Visit` implementations for `tracing-subscriber` - Dual-output logging: colorized ANSI stdout + plain-text file append - `std::io::IsTerminal` to detect foreground vs detached daemon - Log level strategy: ERROR=unrecoverable, WARN=recoverable-unexpected, INFO=consequential-single, DEBUG=high-frequency/expected - NIP-29 / Nostr relay protocol, ordinal identity allocation (`smith`, `smith1`, `smith2`, ...) - `RUST_LOG` env var as the filter override (default `tenex_edge=info`) - `clap` alias to expose `daemon` command while keeping `__daemon` alias for auto-spawner 3. Files and Code Sections: - **`Cargo.toml`** - Added two dependencies: ```toml tracing = "0.1" tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter", "fmt"] } ``` - **`src/logging.rs`** (NEW FILE â€” fully rewritten after initial version) - Custom `Fields` visitor that collects message and key-value pairs separately - `DaemonFormatter` impl of `FormatEvent<S, N>` using owo-colors for rich output - When ANSI: dim gray timestamp, colored level badge with background (` INF ` on cyan black-text, ` WRN ` on yellow, ` ERR ` on red, ` DBG ` on bright_black), bold white message, dim key names, bright_cyan field values - When non-ANSI (file): plain `HH:MM:SS LEVEL message key=value` - `pub fn init_daemon_logging(log_path: &Path) -> Result<()>` â€” installs two layers when stdout is a terminal (colored stdout + plain file), single plain layer when detached - Key imports fix: `use tracing_subscriber::fmt::{format::Writer, layer, FmtContext, FormatEvent, FormatFields}` (avoids `fmt` name collision with `std::fmt`) - **`src/lib.rs`** - Added `pub mod logging;` between `pub mod domain;` and `pub mod identity;` - **`src/cli/args.rs`** - Changed `__daemon` hidden variant to visible `daemon` subcommand with alias: ```rust /// Start the per-machine daemon in the foreground... #[command(name = "daemon", alias = "__daemon")] Daemon, ``` - The auto-spawner in `spawn.rs` still passes `"__daemon"` which resolves via alias - **`src/daemon/server/lifecycle.rs`** - Added at top of `run()`: ```rust crate::logging::init_daemon_logging(&crate::daemon::log_path())?; ``` - Replaced all `eprintln!` with tracing: socket bound â†’ `tracing::info!`, relay connected â†’ `tracing::info!`, warmup complete â†’ `tracing::info!`, spawn-on-mention coverage â†’ `tracing::info!`, initial resubscribe failure â†’ `tracing::warn!`, shutdown â†’ `tracing::info!`, protocol re-exec â†’ `tracing::info!`, connection error â†’ `tracing::debug!`, accept error â†’ `tracing::error!` - **`src/daemon/server/demux.rs`** - `handle_incoming`: replaced `eprintln!` with `tracing::debug!(kind, id, from, "incoming event")` - First-sight logic: `tracing::info!` for most kinds, but `tracing::debug!` for kind:30315 status heartbeats (added `is_heartbeat` check) - Added offline-agent-mention dispatch log at `tracing::info!` - Added orchestration dispatch log at `tracing::info!` - `handle_offline_agent_mention`: replaced all `eprintln!` with structured tracing (has_aliveâ†’debug, resumeâ†’info, resume failâ†’warn, not-memberâ†’debug, ordinal provisioningâ†’info, mgmt add_member failâ†’warn, no pathâ†’warn, spawningâ†’info, spawn outcomeâ†’info/warn) - `handle_orchestration`: replaced debug-gated and unconditional `eprintln!` with tracing::warn (auth rejection, parent mismatch), tracing::debug (already claimed), tracing::info (identity mint, spawn success), tracing::error (spawn failure) - **`src/daemon/server/engine_lifecycle.rs`** - `spawn_session`: added `tracing::info!(agent, channel, session, "spawning session engine")` at entry; removed debug-gate from task error â†’ `tracing::warn!`; added ordinal removal â†’ `tracing::debug!`; added `tracing::info!(session, "session engine exited")` - `ensure_subscription`: added `tracing::debug!(channel, req_count, "opening narrow channel REQs")`; timeout â†’ `tracing::warn!` - `replay_channel_chat`: added `tracing::debug!(channel, "replaying channel chat")`; timeout â†’ `tracing::warn!` - `reconcile_sessions`: added count log at entry; dead session â†’ `tracing::warn!`; reviving â†’ `tracing::info!`; replaced debug-gated `eprintln!` for signer/admission/subscription failures with `tracing::warn!` - **`src/daemon/server/session_start.rs`** - Added `tracing::debug!(agent, harness, channel, "session_start hook received")` after harness is inferred - Stale session cancellation: added `tracing::info!` with reason (same_pid/same_pane) - Idempotent re-assert: added `tracing::info!(agent, session, channel, "session re-assert: engine already running")` - `ensure_channel_ready` timeout: removed debug-gate, â†’ `tracing::warn!` - Subscription failure: `tracing::warn!` replacing debug-gated `eprintln!` - Final session started: `tracing::info!(agent, channel, session, codename, harness, "session started")` - **`src/identity.rs`** - Key loaded from disk: `tracing::debug!(slug, pubkey = %&stored.public_key[..8], "agent key loaded")` - Key created first time: `tracing::info!(slug, pubkey = %&stored.public_key[..8], path = %path.display(), "agent key created")` - **`src/daemon/server/session_signer.rs`** - `select_and_reserve`: reassert path â†’ `tracing::debug!(session, agent, h, ordinal, "ordinal reasserted")`; preferred ordinal occupied â†’ `tracing::warn!` with preferred value; fresh allocation â†’ `tracing::info!(session, agent, h, ordinal, label, "ordinal slot allocated")` - `release`: collect freed slots before retain, then `tracing::info!(session, h, ordinal, "ordinal slot released")` for each - **`src/daemon/server/background/idle.rs`** - Grace period start: `tracing::info!(grace_secs, "daemon idle; starting grace-period countdown")` - Exit: `tracing::info!("grace period elapsed; daemon exiting")` - **`src/relay_log.rs`** - Changed `log_entry` to route through `tracing::debug!("{line}")` instead of `eprintln!`, still writes timestamped line to relay.log file - Effect: relay event lines (previously noisy `[â†’relay]` unformatted stderr) now only show with `RUST_LOG=tenex_edge=debug` - **`src/daemon/server/diagnostics.rs`** - `log_nip29_role_decision`: replaced `eprintln!` with `tracing::debug!(group, target, role, reason, "nip29 role decision")` - **`src/daemon/server/tmux_rpc.rs`** - `could not resolve identity` â†’ `tracing::warn!(slug, error, "tmux_spawn: could not resolve identity")` - Already live concurrent instance â†’ `tracing::info!(slug, scope, pubkey, "tmux_spawn: launching concurrent instance")` - Pre-provisioning channel â†’ `tracing::info!(slug, scope, pubkey, "tmux_spawn: pre-provisioning channel")` - Channel provisioning timeout â†’ `tracing::warn!(scope, "tmux_spawn: channel provisioning timed out; proceeding")` 4. Errors and Fixes: - **`fmt` name collision in logging.rs**: Initial code had both `use std::fmt::{self, ...}` and `use tracing_subscriber::fmt::{self, ...}` causing E0252 "name defined multiple times". Also `fmt::layer()` couldn't be found. - **Fix**: Changed to `use std::fmt;` (no self-import for tracing_subscriber) and `use tracing_subscriber::fmt::{format::Writer, layer, FmtContext, FormatEvent, FormatFields}` (importing `layer` function directly), then replaced `fmt::layer()` with `layer()` throughout. 5. Problem Solving: - **Dual-output logging**: Solved with `std::io::IsTerminal::is_terminal()` â€” when foreground, two separate `layer()` instances (one ANSI stdout, one plain file with `Mutex<File>`); when detached, single plain stdout layer (the fd redirect already delivers to file). - **Rich colors without noise**: Used a custom `Fields` visitor to separate message from key-value pairs, then rendered each part with different owo-colors styling (bold white message, dim keys, bright_cyan values, level-colored background badges). - **Relay log noise**: Routed `relay_log::log_entry` through `tracing::debug!` so relay event lines are suppressed by default (info level) but available with `RUST_LOG=tenex_edge=debug`. - **Status heartbeat noise**: kind:30315 events fire every ~30s; downgraded `first-sight` log for them to `debug` only. - **`__daemon` auto-spawner compatibility**: Used clap `alias = "__daemon"` on the renamed `daemon` command so existing spawn code passing `"__daemon"` still works without modification. 6. All User Messages: - **Message 1**: "I need more logging whenever the daemon does something, when it routes to a session, when it starts a new agent, why it started it, when it creates a new ordinal, why it created it, etc, etc -- use 5 sonnet parallel agents to decide stuff like this to log -- ideally I'll be able to run `tenex-edge daemon` and see colorized output on stdout in addition to seeing the same output written to a daemon.log file" - **Message 2** (with screenshot): "this is very light on colors and on usefulness to be honest" â€” showed screenshot of daemon output with plain `[â†’relay]` lines mixed with lightly-colored tracing lines; only the LEVEL badge was colored; field values were plain white. 7. Level badges now have colored backgrounds (` INF ` on cyan, ` WRN ` on yellow, ` ERR ` on red, ` DBG ` on bright_black). Message text is `bold().white()`. Field values are `.bright_cyan()`. Field keys are `.dimmed()`. Timestamp is `.dimmed()`. **relay_log.rs**: Changed `log_entry` from `eprintln!("{full}")` to `tracing::debug!("{line}")`, still writes to relay.log file. This eliminates the noisy `2026-06-29 11:06:39.xxx [â†’relay]` lines from foreground output at default log level. **diagnostics.rs**: Converted `log_nip29_role_decision` from `eprintln!` to `tracing::debug!` with structured fields. **tmux_rpc.rs**: Converted three `eprintln!` calls to `tracing::info!`/`tracing::warn!`. **demux.rs**: Changed `first-sight` log to `tracing::debug!` for kind:30315 (status heartbeats that fire every 30s), keeping `tracing::info!` for other event kinds. **Compile error fixed**: The `fmt` namespace collision was fixed by using `layer` imported directly from `tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer` and removing the duplicate `self` import.
